## Code Review

This pull request introduces several features, including palm rejection, a distraction-free mode toggled by a three-finger tap, a daily notes manager that can auto-open today's note on startup, and support for favorites and recent files. The review feedback highlights a critical deadlock where enabling palm rejection blocks the three-finger tap gesture, trapping users in distraction-free mode. Additionally, it is recommended to remove the static `didAutoOpenDaily` variable to ensure daily notes reliably auto-open on fresh launches even if the application process was cached in the background.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> The [consumer version of Gemini Code Assist on GitHub](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/review-repo-code) is being sunset. Starting **June 18, 2026**, new organization installations will be blocked, and all code review activity will officially cease on **July 17, 2026**.
> For more details on the timeline and next steps, please review the [Help Documentation](https://developers.google.com/gemini-code-assist/docs/deprecations/consumer-code-review).

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Using a static companion variable `didAutoOpenDaily` to track whether the daily note has been opened will persist as long as the application process is alive in the background. If the user finishes the `MainActivity` (e.g., by pressing back) and later re-opens the app from the launcher while the process is still cached, the daily note will not auto-open. Relying solely on `savedInstanceState == null` is sufficient and correct to prevent auto-opening on configuration changes while ensuring it triggers on every fresh launch.

```kotlin
        if (savedInstanceState == null &&
            PreferencesManager.isOpenDailyOnStartEnabled(this)
        ) {
```

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

Since `didAutoOpenDaily` is no longer needed to track the auto-open state, this companion object can be safely removed to clean up unused code.

![medium](https://www.gstatic.com/codereviewagent/medium-priority.svg)

```kotlin
        ) {
```
